title: Human Impacts Institute 2026 Artivism and Amplifiers Fellowship: Paid Opportunity for Artists and Climate Changemakers
summary: The Human Impacts Institute is now accepting applications for its 2026 Artivism and Amplifiers Cohort , a paid fellowship for artists, activists, and communicators using creativity to drive climate action and social change. This unique program supports individuals who combine art and activism to educate communities, amplify underrepresented voices, and inspire environmental justice solutions. About the Artivism and Amplifiers Program The Artivism and Amplifiers Fellowship equips participants with the tools, mentorship, and community needed to strengthen their impact through storytelling, artistic expression, and advocacy. Participants join a global network of changemakers who use art as a catalyst for awareness, dialogue, and action on climate and sustainability issues. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows receive: Paid fellowship support Training in climate communication and advocacy Mentorship from experts and practitioners Access to a global community of artists and activists Opportunities to collaborate on impactful projects Enhanced visibility for their work Program Focus Areas The fellowship is designed for those working in areas such as: Climate justice Environmental storytelling Community engagement Creative advocacy Social and environmental education Who Should Apply This fellowship is ideal for: Artists using their work to promote social and environmental change Community organizers integrating creativity into advocacy Educators and storytellers focused on climate action Activists seeking new tools to amplify their message Why You Should Apply The Human Impacts Institute Artivism and Amplifiers Fellowship is an outstanding opportunity to be compensated for your creative and advocacy work while gaining specialized training and joining a supportive international network. If you are passionate about leveraging art to inspire climate solutions and social justice, this program can help expand your reach and deepen your impact. How to Apply Learn more and submit your application through the official fellowship page. For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Wellcome Accelerator Awards 2026: Funding Opportunity for Early and Mid-Career Researchers of Black, Bangladeshi, and Pakistani Heritage in the United Kingdom
summary: The Wellcome Accelerator Awards is a targeted funding initiative designed to support early-career and mid-career researchers in the United Kingdom who identify as being from Black, Bangladeshi, or Pakistani heritage backgrounds. The scheme is delivered by Wellcome and aims to address underrepresentation in research funding while strengthening career progression within academia and health-related research fields. This award provides flexible financial and professional development support to help researchers advance to the next stage of their academic or research careers. It allows recipients to engage in research and research-adjacent activities such as training, secondments, networking, and pilot research projects. Overview of the Wellcome Accelerator Awards The Accelerator Awards are structured to provide both financial support and career development opportunities for researchers working within the UK research ecosystem. The scheme runs annually until 2028 and is highly competitive, focusing on career progression and research impact. Key Funding Details Funding body: Wellcome Funding duration: Up to 2 years Funding amount: Up to £200,000 for project expenses plus salary (if required) Application deadline: 25 August 2026 at 15:00 BST Webinar date: 2 June 2026 at 14:00 BST Application platform: Wellcome Funding Platform Award frequency: Annual (until 2028) Co-applicants: Not permitted Purpose of the Award The Wellcome Accelerator Awards are designed to: Address inequalities in research funding access Support career progression for underrepresented researchers Strengthen academic and research leadership pathways Enable researchers to build competitive profiles for future funding opportunities Support both research and career development activities By the end of the award period, researchers are expected to be better positioned for promotions, major research grants, and leadership roles in academia or research institutions. Eligibility Criteria To apply for the Wellcome Accelerator Awards, applicants must meet strict eligibility requirements. 1. Heritage Requirement Applicants must self-identify as being from one of the following backgrounds: Black heritage (including mixed Black heritage such as Black African, Black Caribbean, or other Black backgrounds) Bangladeshi heritage (including mixed Bangladeshi heritage) Pakistani heritage (including mixed Pakistani heritage) 2. Location Requirement Applicants must: Be currently working in or ordinarily resident in the United Kingdom Hold a position at a UK-based administering organisation 3. Academic and Research Training Requirement Applicants must have completed a substantive period of research training, such as: A PhD or equivalent higher research degree OR completion of a PhD viva by the deadline (if still studying) OR at least four years of equivalent research experience (for non-PhD pathways) Additionally, applicants must have: An undergraduate degree or PhD awarded by a UK institution 4. Employment and Career Stage Conditions Applicants must: Be early-career or mid-career researchers Have been appointed to a permanent or long-term role within the last 3 years (with some exceptions for career breaks, part-time work, or clinical roles) Not hold another active Wellcome award as a lead applicant 5. Time Commitment Requirements At least 80% of research time must be dedicated to the grant Salary-supported applicants should limit non-research duties to: Maximum 20% (general roles) Up to 40% for clinical specialists Scope of Activities Funded The award is highly flexible and supports both research and career-building activities. Eligible Research Activities Pilot studies and proof-of-concept research Scoping studies and preliminary data collection Writing research papers or grant applications Collaborative research projects Eligible Research-Adjacent Activities Advanced skills training and professional development courses Research secondments (UK or international) Networking activities and academic conferences Industry or NGO placements (e.g., pharmaceutical companies or research organizations) Collaborative research meetings Funding Coverage The award may cover a wide range of costs, including: Salary support (if requested) Research materials and consumables Travel and fieldwork expenses Equipment and access charges Training and career development costs Open access publication fees Clinical research costs (where applicable) Overheads and institutional support costs Relocation and overseas allowances Public engagement and patient involvement activities Application Process Step 1: Preparation Applicants must prepare: Career goals and long-term vision Detailed project plan Skills development plan Budget justification Step 2: Submission Applications must be submitted via the Wellcome Funding Platform and approved by the host institution. APPLY HERE Step 3: Institutional Approval Applications must be reviewed and approved by the administering organisation Institutions must ensure compliance with funding requirements Late submissions are not accepted Assessment Process Shortlisting Criteria Applications are assessed based on: Career progression plans and long-term academic goals Contribution to research culture Alignment of proposed activities with career development Final Selection Criteria Career vision and ambitions (35%) Project feasibility and quality (35%) Applicant track record and contributions (30%) Timeline of Key Dates Applications open: 6 May 2026 Information webinar: 2 June 2026 Full application deadline: 25 August 2026 (15:00 BST) Shortlisting: October 2026 Final funding decisions: Mid-December 2026 Webinar Information An information webinar will be held on 2 June 2026 at 14:00 BST. The session will include: Guidance from the Wellcome funding team Insights from previous award recipients Advice from selection committee members Application writing tips Additional Information Career breaks, maternity/paternity leave, and illness are considered during assessment Part-time researchers are eligible Applicants can resubmit if previously unsuccessful (with revisions) Institutional support is mandatory for application success Conclusion The Wellcome Accelerator Awards represent a significant opportunity for researchers from Black, Bangladeshi, and Pakistani heritage backgrounds in the UK to advance their academic careers. By combining flexible funding with structured career development, the scheme is designed to strengthen representation and support long-term success in research and academia. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Wellcome Accelerator Awards 2026: Funding Opportunity for Early and Mid-Career Researchers of Black, Bangladeshi, and Pakistani Heritage in the United Kingdom appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: LEGO Group Student Worker Opportunity 2026: Talent Acquisition Role in Billund, Denmark
summary: The LEGO Group is hiring a Student Worker in Talent Acquisition at its global headquarters in Billund, Denmark . This exciting opportunity is ideal for university students who want to gain hands-on experience in recruitment and human resources while working for one of the world’s most admired and innovative companies. The role offers flexible working hours of 15–20 hours per week , making it an excellent opportunity to build professional skills while continuing your studies. About the Opportunity As a Student Worker in Talent Acquisition, you will support recruitment activities for trainees, apprentices, and hourly manufacturing employees. You will work closely with the Talent Acquisition team and help shape the future workforce of the LEGO Group. This position provides valuable exposure to corporate recruitment processes and offers practical experience in one of the most dynamic areas of Human Resources. Key Responsibilities The selected student worker will: Assist with recruitment coordination and candidate communication Support hiring for trainees, apprentices, and manufacturing roles Help schedule interviews and maintain recruitment systems Collaborate with the Talent Acquisition team Contribute to creating a positive candidate experience Benefits of Working at LEGO Group Hands-on experience in Talent Acquisition and HR Flexible part-time schedule (15–20 hours weekly) Opportunity to work at LEGO Group headquarters in Billund Exposure to global recruitment practices Professional growth and networking opportunities Experience with a world-renowned employer brand Ideal Candidate Profile LEGO Group is looking for a student who is: Currently pursuing a degree in HR or a related field Proactive and organized A strong communicator Interested in recruitment and people operations Able to balance studies and part-time work effectively Why You Should Apply Working as a Student Worker at LEGO Group offers an excellent entry point into Human Resources and Talent Acquisition. You will gain practical, resume-enhancing experience while contributing to recruitment efforts at one of the world’s most iconic brands. For students aspiring to careers in HR, recruitment, or organizational development, this role provides valuable real-world exposure in an international corporate setting. Work Schedule Hours: 15–20 hours per week Location: Billund, Denmark Format: On-site with flexibility How to Apply Submit your application through the official LEGO Group careers page . For More Job Opportunities , Visit OFY here. The post LEGO Group Student Worker Opportunity 2026: Talent Acquisition Role in Billund, Denmark appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: FOSI Digital Safety Fellow 2026–2027: Graduate Fellowship in Online Safety and Technology Policy ($10,000 Grant)
summary: The Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) is now accepting applications for the Digital Safety Fellow 2026–2027 , an exciting graduate fellowship for students interested in emerging technology, digital policy, online safety, and youth wellbeing. This highly competitive fellowship offers a $10,000 grant award and the opportunity to work with one of the world’s leading organizations focused on making the online world safer for children and families. The fellowship runs from September 2026 to May 2027 and is based in a hybrid format in Washington, D.C. About the Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) FOSI is an international nonprofit organization that works to make the internet safer for children and families through research, policy advocacy, and industry collaboration. The organization partners with technology companies, policymakers, educators, and parents to address issues such as: Digital wellbeing Child online protection Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies Social media safety Privacy and cybersecurity Fellowship Overview The Digital Safety Fellow will help bridge the gap between technology and policy by testing and evaluating newly launched technologies, assessing their impact on children and families, and translating findings into accessible content that informs FOSI’s research, advocacy, and communications efforts. Fellowship Details Host Organization: Family Online Safety Institute (FOSI) Program Type: Graduate Fellowship Location: Hybrid | Washington, D.C., USA Duration: September 2026 – May 2027 Grant Award: $10,000 Application Status: Open Responsibilities The selected fellow will: Test and evaluate emerging technologies and AI tools Research their implications for children and families Produce accessible reports, articles, and educational content Contribute to policy and advocacy initiatives Support communications and public outreach efforts Benefits of the Fellowship Participants will gain: A $10,000 fellowship grant Practical experience in technology policy and online safety Mentorship from experts in digital wellbeing Exposure to research and advocacy work Opportunities to contribute to high-impact policy discussions Professional networking in Washington, D.C. Ideal Candidate Profile FOSI is seeking graduate students who are: Passionate about online safety and youth wellbeing Interested in emerging technologies and AI Skilled in research and analysis Strong writers and communicators Able to translate complex issues into accessible content Why You Should Apply The rapid development of AI, social media, and other digital tools is reshaping childhood and family life. This fellowship offers a unique opportunity to examine these changes firsthand and contribute to meaningful policy and advocacy work that helps protect children online. For students pursuing careers in technology policy, public affairs, cybersecurity, education, or nonprofit leadership, the FOSI Digital Safety Fellowship provides valuable experience and a respected professional credential. Application Timeline Program Period: September 2026 – May 2027 Application Deadline: Check the official FOSI fellowship page for the most current deadline information. How to Apply Learn more and submit your application through the official FOSI website. For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: U.S. Department of the Treasury Fall 2026 Internship Program: Gain Hands-On Experience in Finance, Policy, and National Security
summary: Applications are now open for the U.S. Department of the Treasury Fall 2026 Internship Program , an exceptional opportunity for students interested in public service, finance, economics, policy, and national security. Hosted by the U.S. Department of the Treasury and administered through USAJOBS , this internship offers current high school, undergraduate, and graduate students the chance to work directly with one of the most influential federal agencies in the United States. Interns will gain practical experience supporting Treasury’s mission to promote economic prosperity and safeguard the financial security of the United States. About the Internship Program The Treasury Departmental Offices Internship Program provides substantive assignments related to students’ academic interests and career goals. Interns work alongside experienced professionals on projects involving finance, public policy, economics, law, information technology, and government operations. The Fall 2026 internship begins in September 2026 and requires a commitment of at least 20 hours per week for a minimum of 10 consecutive weeks . Internship Benefits Participants will have the opportunity to: Gain hands-on experience in federal government Develop leadership, analytical, and management skills Build professional networks with Treasury staff and policymakers Explore career pathways in public service Contribute to meaningful policy and operational initiatives Sample Responsibilities Intern duties may include: Collecting, analyzing, and presenting data Conducting policy and economic research Drafting briefing materials and reports Attending meetings and congressional hearings Organizing high-level events Supporting budgeting, strategic planning, IT, and communications functions Eligible Fields of Study Students from a wide range of academic disciplines are encouraged to apply, including: Accounting and Finance Economics and Mathematics Political Science and Government International Affairs Law and Legal Studies Computer Science and Information Technology Journalism and Communications Public Administration Security and Intelligence Studies STEM and Cybersecurity Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be a U.S. citizen Be currently enrolled at least half-time in an accredited high school, college, or graduate institution Maintain a minimum 2.0 GPA on a 4.0 scale Be in good academic standing Be available to begin in September 2026 Commit to at least 20 hours per week Complete a background investigation Work in person in Washington, DC Cover their own housing and relocation costs if selected Compensation This is a volunteer (unpaid) internship . Salary: $0 No relocation or housing assistance is provided Despite being unpaid, the internship offers substantial exposure to federal policy and government operations. Required Application Materials Applicants must submit: Resume (maximum two pages) Current transcript (unofficial accepted) Proof of enrollment or acceptance Cover letter (optional) Application Deadline Applications close on: 31 May 2026 at 11:59 PM Eastern Time How to Apply Apply through USAJOBS. For more Internship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: University of Birmingham Global South Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Early-Career Researchers from the Global South
summary: The University of Birmingham has officially opened applications for the prestigious Global South Fellowship Program 2026, an international fellowship initiative designed to support the professional growth and research development of early-career researchers from universities across the Global South. This highly competitive fellowship programme offers selected fellows the opportunity to spend up to three months at the University of Birmingham in the United Kingdom, where they will engage in collaborative research, gain insight into world-class academic systems, strengthen institutional partnerships, and build long-term international collaborations. The fellowship is aimed at scholars and researchers working in areas connected to: Education access and equity Global health Sustainability and climate action However, the programme also provides some flexibility for applicants from related disciplines and interdisciplinary research areas. The initiative is part of a broader commitment by the University of Birmingham and the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign to strengthen global academic partnerships and support research collaboration with institutions in the Global South. Successful fellows will receive: Travel funding of up to £850 A monthly stipend of £1500 Access to academic mentorship and collaboration opportunities Research engagement at one of the UK’s leading universities Applications are currently open, and interested candidates must apply before the deadline of May 29, 2026. About the Global South Fellowship Program The Global South Fellowship Program is an international visiting fellowship initiative focused on strengthening academic collaboration between researchers from the Global South and leading global institutions. The programme enables early-career researchers to: Develop professionally Engage in collaborative research Build international academic partnerships Learn from global research systems Expand institutional networks Strengthen research capacity The fellowship also aims to promote deeper collaboration between universities in the Global South and major international research institutions. The programme is jointly connected with: The University of Birmingham The University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign Selected fellows will gain exposure to: Research administration Academic leadership Research methodologies International research collaboration Higher education systems Objectives of the Fellowship Programme The Global South Fellowship Program was created to strengthen research partnerships and support sustainable academic collaboration globally. The programme specifically aims to: Support the professional development of early-career researchers Promote international research collaboration Build long-term academic partnerships Strengthen institutional cooperation with the Global South Enhance research skills and academic leadership Facilitate knowledge exchange between institutions Encourage interdisciplinary collaboration Support global research engagement in priority development areas The fellowship places strong emphasis on research that contributes to global development and social impact. Priority Research Areas The University of Birmingham welcomes applications from researchers working in several priority fields. 1. Education Access and Equity Researchers focusing on: Inclusive education Education policy Access to learning Educational technology Social inclusion Equity in higher education Educational development are encouraged to apply. 2. Global Health Applicants working in: Public health Global disease prevention Health systems strengthening Healthcare innovation Epidemiology Community health Health equity may qualify for the fellowship. 3. Sustainability and Climate Action The fellowship also supports research related to: Climate change Environmental sustainability Renewable energy Sustainable development Climate resilience Green innovation Environmental policy Flexibility in Research Areas Although the programme prioritizes the above fields, the University of Birmingham notes that there is flexibility around subject areas. This means applicants from: Interdisciplinary fields Emerging research areas Related development-focused disciplines may also be considered if their work aligns with the broader goals of the programme. Fellowship Funding and Financial Support The Global South Fellowship Program offers financial support to help selected fellows cover major fellowship-related expenses. Travel Funding Each fellow will receive: Up to £850 for travel expenses This funding is intended to support: International airfare Travel-related logistics Transportation costs associated with the fellowship Monthly Stipend Selected fellows will also receive: £1500 per month For up to three months The stipend is intended to help cover: Accommodation Living expenses Daily costs during the fellowship period Accommodation Support The University of Birmingham may assist fellows with accommodation arrangements. Key accommodation details include: On-campus accommodation may be booked on behalf of fellows Accommodation costs may be deducted from the stipend University accommodation availability is not guaranteed Additional guidance on private accommodation options is available upon request The university also provides support to help fellows organize suitable housing during their stay. Fellowship Duration The fellowship period runs between: July 2026 and June 2027 However: Funding support is available for a maximum of three months during this period Selected fellows may coordinate with their academic hosts to determine suitable fellowship dates. Benefits of the Global South Fellowship Program The fellowship provides substantial academic, professional, and networking benefits. 1. International Research Experience Fellows gain direct exposure to: UK academic systems International research environments Advanced research practices Global academic collaboration 2. Research Collaboration Opportunities Participants can: Build research partnerships Collaborate with leading academics Explore joint publications Develop future grant opportunities 3. Professional Development The programme helps fellows strengthen: Research skills Academic leadership International engagement Research administration knowledge 4. Networking Opportunities Fellows become part of a broader academic and research network involving: International scholars University researchers Academic departments Research institutes 5. Exposure to Global Research Systems Participants gain practical understanding of: Research organization University administration Academic collaboration systems International higher education structures Who Can Apply? The fellowship is intended for: Early-career researchers from universities in the Global South The programme recognizes that the definition of an early-career researcher may differ across disciplines and regions. However, there is one mandatory requirement: Applicants must have completed a doctorate degree Academic Eligibility Requirement To qualify, applicants must: Hold a completed doctoral degree (PhD or equivalent) The programme does not impose a rigid number of years since completion of the doctorate, allowing flexibility across disciplines and academic career paths. Finding a Research Host Applicants are encouraged to identify a suitable host researcher at the University of Birmingham before submitting their applications. The university advises candidates to: Search university staff directories Identify researchers with aligned academic interests Contact potential hosts directly Discuss possible collaboration opportunities Explore hosting arrangements Securing a strong host relationship may strengthen the fellowship application. Application Timeline Application Deadline: May 29, 2026 Application Results Announcement: June 25, 2026 Fellowship Period: July 2026 – June 2027 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and begin discussions with potential hosts as soon as possible. Why This Fellowship Matters The Global South Fellowship Program plays a significant role in strengthening equitable global academic collaboration. Researchers from the Global South often face: Limited funding opportunities Restricted international mobility Barriers to global research participation Unequal access to international academic networks This programme seeks to address those challenges by creating opportunities for: Knowledge exchange Institutional partnership International collaboration Capacity building Global research engagement The fellowship also contributes to advancing research in areas critical to sustainable global development. How to Apply Interested applicants should: Identify a potential academic host at the University of Birmingham Prepare fellowship application materials Demonstrate alignment between their research and programme goals Submit the application before the deadline Applicants are encouraged to highlight: Research achievements Academic publications International collaboration interests Development impact of their research Long-term partnership goals Applications should be submitted through the official University of Birmingham fellowship application platform. Apply HERE Fore more information, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post University of Birmingham Global South Fellowship Program 2026: Fully Funded Research Fellowship for Early-Career Researchers from the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA Dexterous Robotics Internship 2026 at Johnson Space Center: Fully Funded Hands-On Robotics and Space Exploration Opportunity for Students in the United States
summary: NASA has officially opened applications for the highly competitive Dexterous Robotics – Special Projects Internship for Fall 2026 at the Johnson Space Center (JSC) in Houston, Texas. This prestigious in-person internship opportunity offers undergraduate and graduate students the chance to work directly on advanced robotics systems designed to support future lunar missions, autonomous space operations, and next-generation space exploration technologies. The internship is hosted under NASA’s Robotics Technology Development Branch and focuses on developing cutting-edge robotic manipulation systems capable of supporting future operations on the Moon and beyond. Interns selected for this opportunity will gain hands-on experience in robotics engineering, embedded systems, autonomous systems, avionics, control algorithms, software development, and high-performance computing. This internship represents an extraordinary opportunity for students passionate about robotics, aerospace engineering, computer science, artificial intelligence, embedded systems, automation, and space exploration to contribute directly to real NASA projects shaping the future of human and robotic exploration. Applications are currently open, and interested students must apply before the registration deadline of May 22, 2026. About the NASA Dexterous Robotics Internship The Dexterous Robotics – Special Projects Internship is part of NASA’s internship programme aimed at providing educational and operational experiences for students interested in STEM careers and aerospace innovation. The internship is hosted by the Robotics Technology Development Branch at NASA Johnson Space Center, one of NASA’s most important centers for human spaceflight, robotics, astronaut operations, and advanced exploration technologies. Interns participating in this programme will work alongside NASA engineers, researchers, and technical specialists on projects focused on robotic manipulation systems for future space missions. NASA’s Vision for Future Robotics and Lunar Exploration NASA is actively advancing robotic systems capable of supporting long-duration lunar and deep-space missions. Future space exploration missions require highly capable robotic technologies that can assist astronauts and operate autonomously during uncrewed periods. The robotics systems being developed at Johnson Space Center are expected to support critical space mission operations such as: Lunar site preparation Infrastructure support Scientific exploration Logistics and cargo handling Maintenance operations Human-robot collaboration Autonomous robotic missions Rover coordination Satellite manipulation systems The internship programme gives students the opportunity to contribute directly to technologies designed for future Moon and space missions. Internship Focus Areas The internship combines both hardware and software development for advanced robotic systems. Selected interns will contribute to: Robotics software engineering Autonomous systems development Flight-qualified software systems Embedded systems engineering Sensor and actuator integration Robotics control systems Dynamic system modeling FPGA and MCU programming Avionics design High-performance computing systems The programme bridges the gap between current terrestrial robotics technologies and future space robotics systems. Key Responsibilities and Internship Activities Interns selected for this opportunity may participate in a wide range of technical and research activities. 1. Robotics Hardware Development Interns may assist with: Designing robotics hardware systems Developing robotic manipulation components Integrating motors, sensors, and actuators Testing robotic systems and subsystems Supporting avionics hardware design This work contributes directly to next-generation robotic manipulation technologies for space applications. 2. Embedded Systems and Firmware Development Participants may work on: FPGA programming MCU software development Embedded systems integration Linux driver development Hardware description language programming Students will gain exposure to flight-oriented embedded systems and real-world robotics engineering workflows. 3. Robotics Software and Control Systems Interns may contribute to: ROS 2 development using C++ or Python Robotics control algorithm design Autonomous manipulation systems Dynamic modeling and simulations Flight-qualified software systems This area of work is critical for enabling intelligent robotic operations in space environments. 4. Autonomous Systems and Human-Robot Interaction NASA’s future missions require safe and effective collaboration between astronauts and robotic systems. Interns may support: Human-robot interaction systems Autonomous robotics coordination Rover-manipulator synchronization Safety-focused robotic operations Multi-system robotic communication Learning Opportunities and Technical Experience The internship is designed to provide extensive technical learning and hands-on engineering experience. Interns will: Gain practical robotics engineering experience Work on real NASA projects Learn advanced robotics software systems Build expertise in autonomous systems Understand robotic systems for space exploration Improve systems engineering capabilities Enhance problem-solving and technical communication skills By the end of the internship, participants will have significantly strengthened their technical and professional abilities in robotics and aerospace engineering. Ideal Technical Skills and Qualifications NASA is seeking students with strong academic backgrounds and technical skills in robotics, engineering, and software systems. Preferred Technical Skills Applicants with experience in the following areas are highly encouraged to apply: PCB Design Altium PCB design tools Electronics systems integration Robotics hardware development Robotics Integration and Testing Sensors and actuators Motors and gear-trains Robotics hardware validation Embedded Systems Development FPGA programming MCU software development Linux driver programming Firmware engineering Hardware Description Languages Experience with: VHDL Verilog SystemVerilog Robotics Programming ROS 2 C++ Python Robotics Modeling and Simulation MATLAB Python-based modeling Dynamic simulations Flight Software Systems Core Flight System (cFS) Flight-qualified software engineering Important Nontechnical Skills NASA also values several professional and collaborative competencies. Applicants should demonstrate: Strong analytical thinking Creativity and innovation Team collaboration abilities Communication and presentation skills Problem-solving capabilities Ability to work in multidisciplinary environments Internship Location and Work Structure Internship Location Houston, Texas, United States NASA Center Johnson Space Center (JSC) Internship Format In-person Work Schedule Full-Time This internship offers students the opportunity to work directly within NASA’s engineering and robotics environment. Academic Levels Eligible to Apply NASA is accepting applications from: Undergraduate Juniors Undergraduate Seniors Master’s Students Doctoral Students Students from multiple engineering and technical disciplines are encouraged to apply. Preferred Academic Majors The internship is especially suitable for students studying: Computer Science Aerospace Engineering Computer Engineering Electrical Engineering Mechanical Engineering Mechatronics Engineering Robotics Engineering Automation Engineering Electronics Engineering Students in related STEM fields with relevant technical experience may also qualify. Citizenship Requirement This internship opportunity is open only to: United States Citizens Applicants must meet NASA’s citizenship eligibility requirements before applying. Internship Session Internship Period Fall 2026 Application Timeline Registration Start Date : March 16, 2026 Registration Deadline: May 22, 2026 Students are strongly encouraged to submit applications early due to the highly competitive nature of NASA internships. Why This NASA Internship Matters NASA internships are among the most respected STEM opportunities globally. The Dexterous Robotics Internship offers students direct exposure to: Space robotics Autonomous systems Lunar mission technologies Advanced aerospace systems Robotics engineering research Human space exploration technologies The internship also helps students build professional networks and gain experience that can support future careers in: Aerospace engineering Robotics Artificial intelligence Autonomous systems Space technology Embedded systems Research and development This opportunity is especially valuable for students seeking careers at NASA, aerospace companies, robotics firms, or advanced technology organizations. How to Apply Interested applicants should apply through the official NASA internship portal. Applicants are encouraged to prepare: Updated resume Academic records Technical project experience Relevant engineering and programming skills Research experience Robotics portfolio (if available) Apply HERE For more information, visit the official website Discover more global internship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post NASA Dexterous Robotics Internship 2026 at Johnson Space Center: Fully Funded Hands-On Robotics and Space Exploration Opportunity for Students in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Postdoctoral Research Fellow Positions on Climate Loss and Damage in the Pacific 2026: Five-Year Research Opportunity for Environmental Social Scientists
summary: Applications are now open for two prestigious Postdoctoral Research Fellow positions linked to a flagship ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship focused on climate-driven loss and damage in the Pacific region. These rare five-year research-focused roles provide an exceptional opportunity for scholars interested in climate change, environmental social science, Pacific governance, cultural continuity, and community-based research. The positions are deeply grounded in reciprocal partnerships with communities and policy stakeholders across the Pacific, particularly in the Cook Islands, Fiji, and Vanuatu. The fellowship initiative places strong emphasis on ethical, relational, and locally led research approaches while addressing some of the most urgent climate-related challenges facing Pacific communities today. Researchers passionate about applied research, policy impact, and community engagement are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline. About the Research Fellowship Opportunity The positions are part of a major ARC Industry Laureate Fellowship programme focused on climate-driven loss and damage in Pacific Island communities. The research initiative seeks to better understand and respond to the social, cultural, and non-economic impacts of climate change across the Pacific region. Unlike many traditional academic roles, these fellowships are highly applied and community-embedded, prioritizing partnerships with local communities and organizations while ensuring that research outputs contribute directly to policy and practice. The programme emphasizes: Reciprocal partnerships Community-led responses Ethical research engagement Policy translation Cultural continuity Non-economic climate loss and damage Locally grounded knowledge systems Number of Positions Available The programme is recruiting: Two Postdoctoral Research Fellows These positions are considered rare long-term opportunities due to their five-year duration and strong research focus. Fellowship Duration Successful applicants will receive: Five-year research appointments This extended timeframe allows fellows to engage deeply with communities, build long-term relationships, and contribute meaningfully to research and policy outcomes. Research Focus Areas The fellowship focuses on climate-driven loss and damage in Pacific Island contexts. Key thematic areas include: 1. Non-Economic Climate Losses Research may explore impacts that go beyond financial or material damages, including: Loss of cultural heritage Displacement impacts Community identity Traditional knowledge systems Spiritual and emotional dimensions of climate change 2. Cultural Continuity The programme emphasizes the importance of preserving and supporting cultural continuity in communities affected by climate change. 3. Community-Led Climate Responses The fellowship values locally driven adaptation and resilience strategies developed in collaboration with Pacific communities. 4. Policy and Practice Translation Researchers will contribute to translating research findings into practical policy recommendations and community-focused outcomes. Countries and Regional Focus The fellowship work is closely connected to communities and partners in: Cook Islands Fiji Vanuatu The broader research context is centered on the Pacific region and Pacific Island communities. Type of Research Environment This opportunity is distinct because of its values-led and relational approach to research. The work is: Community-embedded Collaborative Applied Interdisciplinary Ethically grounded Policy-oriented Researchers will work alongside: Communities Policy partners Practice organizations Regional stakeholders Governance actors Who Should Apply? The programme is particularly interested in scholars working in: Environmental Social Science Climate Change Studies Human Geography Development Studies Anthropology Sociology Environmental Governance Pacific Studies Indigenous Studies Community Development Related interdisciplinary fields Applicants with experience in ethical and relational research practice are especially encouraged to apply. Strong Encouragement for Pacific Islander Scholars The programme strongly encourages applications from: Pacific Islander scholars Researchers deeply connected to Pacific communities Scholars with expertise in Pacific governance and local contexts This emphasis reflects the programme’s commitment to locally grounded and community-centered research approaches. Desired Experience and Skills Ideal candidates are likely to have: Experience working closely with communities Strong qualitative and interdisciplinary research skills Interest in climate policy and applied research Ability to engage in ethical and relational research practices Experience translating research into policy and practice Knowledge of Pacific governance or community contexts Willingness to undertake extended in-country engagement in Pacific communities Why This Opportunity Matters Climate-driven loss and damage is one of the most urgent issues facing Pacific Island nations. Many communities are already experiencing: Rising sea levels Coastal erosion Cultural displacement Threats to livelihoods Environmental degradation Loss of heritage and identity This fellowship programme seeks to support research that is not only academically rigorous but also meaningful, practical, and directly beneficial to affected communities. The initiative recognizes that climate change is not only an environmental challenge but also a cultural, social, and governance issue requiring collaborative and locally informed solutions. Benefits of the Fellowship 1. Long-Term Research Stability The five-year duration provides rare long-term academic and professional stability. 2. Meaningful Community Engagement Researchers will build authentic partnerships with Pacific communities and organizations. 3. Policy Impact Opportunities The programme emphasizes translating research into actionable policy and practice. 4. International and Regional Collaboration Fellows will work with a consortium of partners across the Pacific region. 5. Applied and Purpose-Driven Research The opportunity allows researchers to contribute directly to climate resilience and community wellbeing. Application Deadline Applications for the Postdoctoral Research Fellow positions close on: 21 May 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and carefully review the full position description before submitting their applications. Click here for more and to apply Final Thoughts These Postdoctoral Research Fellow positions represent an exceptional opportunity for scholars committed to ethical, community-centered climate research in the Pacific. With a strong focus on cultural continuity, non-economic climate losses, and locally led responses, the programme offers a rare chance to conduct impactful research while building meaningful partnerships with Pacific communities. Researchers interested in climate justice, environmental social science, Pacific governance, and applied policy work should strongly consider applying before the deadline. title: Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026: Fully Paid Youth Climate Leadership Opportunity in Nairobi, Kenya
summary: Young people across Kenya now have an exciting opportunity to become part of a growing movement focused on climate action, sustainability, and community transformation through the Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026. The program is designed to empower young leaders with climate knowledge, digital literacy, leadership abilities, and practical community engagement skills needed to drive meaningful environmental change. The Climate Champions initiative by Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya (DOT Kenya) is not just another training opportunity. It is a youth-led climate action movement that seeks to mobilize young people to become active changemakers in their communities. Through training, mentorship, participatory research, and climate innovation, selected participants will work directly with communities to co-create and implement local climate solutions. This opportunity comes at a critical time when climate change continues to affect communities across Kenya and beyond. By investing in youth leadership and grassroots innovation, DOT Kenya aims to strengthen community resilience while creating pathways for young people to gain practical experience, leadership exposure, and career-enhancing skills. The application deadline is May 26, 2026 . About Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya is a locally registered non-governmental organization established in 2005 and officially registered in 2007. The organization focuses on empowering underserved and disadvantaged youth with: Digital literacy 21st-century skills Leadership development Innovation and entrepreneurship skills Confidence to thrive in the digital economy DOT Kenya operates as part of the broader DOT Global network, which works across Sub-Saharan Africa, the Middle East, and the United Kingdom. Over the years, the organization has supported thousands of young people in building sustainable livelihoods while becoming leaders in their communities. What is the Digital Opportunity Trust Climate Program? The DOT Climate Program is a youth-centered initiative focused on building a movement of climate-conscious young leaders capable of driving change at the grassroots level. The program is based on the belief that young people are powerful agents of change who can: Influence positive behavior change Mobilize communities toward sustainability Design local climate solutions Promote climate awareness Lead environmental action projects Through the program, selected Climate Champions will receive specialized training and tools that will help them engage communities in climate action and environmental sustainability initiatives. Overview of the Cisco Climate Project The Cisco-supported Climate Champions initiative seeks to scale youth-led climate action across communities in Kenya and Jordan. Through funding support from Cisco, DOT Kenya will recruit and train digitally skilled youth leaders who will serve as Climate Champions in their local communities. The project will equip participants with: DOT Climate Curriculum Participatory Action Research (PAR) Toolkit Community mobilization skills Digital advocacy skills Climate coaching techniques Leadership and communication skills After training, Climate Champions will work directly with communities to identify environmental challenges and co-design practical climate solutions. The project emphasizes local ownership, sustainability, and community participation in addressing climate-related issues. Role of the Climate Champions Selected participants will work closely with DOT Kenya to deliver environmental education and climate awareness initiatives in Nairobi and surrounding areas. As a Climate Champion, participants will help communities: Understand climate change challenges Build resilience Develop sustainable environmental practices Implement community-driven climate solutions This is a short-term but highly impactful leadership opportunity for youth interested in environmental sustainability, climate advocacy, digital innovation, and community development. Program Details Position Title Climate Champion Program Duration 3 Months Location Nairobi, Kenya Reporting To Program Coordinator Training Structure Selected Climate Champions will participate in a 4-week hybrid training program beginning in June 2026. The training will include: Three weeks of virtual learning sessions One week of physical in-person training Participants will receive hands-on learning experiences focused on climate action, participatory research, community engagement, and leadership development. Key Responsibilities of Climate Champions Successful applicants will undertake several important responsibilities during the program. These responsibilities include: 1. Conducting Community Surveys Participants will conduct baseline surveys to better understand local environmental and climate-related challenges within communities. 2. Mobilizing Community Members Climate Champions will engage and coach community members on sustainable environmental practices and climate resilience strategies. 3. Leading Participatory Action Research Groups Participants will form and lead Participatory Action Research (PAR) working groups consisting of 7–9 members. 4. Gathering Community Insights Champions will use participatory methods to gather community perspectives and identify local climate priorities. 5. Co-Designing Climate Solutions Participants will work collaboratively with communities to develop and implement locally driven climate action solutions. 6. Presenting Outcomes Climate Champions will present project outcomes, lessons learned, and climate solutions developed during the program. 7. Reporting and Continuous Learning Participants will submit reports and participate in continuous improvement and learning activities throughout the program period. 8. Global Collaboration Champions will engage with climate leaders and organizations globally through virtual discussions, networking opportunities, and collaborative sessions. 9. Digital Advocacy Participants will strengthen their social media and digital advocacy skills to promote climate awareness and sustainable solutions online. Benefits of the Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program The program offers numerous benefits for selected participants. Monthly Stipends Climate Champions will receive monthly stipends during the program period. Certificate of Completion Participants who successfully complete the program will receive certificates recognizing their participation and achievements. Access to a Global Climate Network Selected youth will gain access to international climate leaders, networks, and collaborations. DOT Alumni Membership Graduates of the program will become members of the DOT Alumni network, opening doors to future opportunities and collaborations. Career Advancement Opportunities Participants will gain practical climate action project experience that can strengthen their professional portfolios and career prospects. Development of 21st-Century Skills The program will help participants strengthen: Communication skills Leadership skills Negotiation abilities Team collaboration skills Critical thinking skills Digital Literacy Skills Climate Champions will improve their knowledge and use of: Social media platforms Digital communication tools Research methodologies Data collection and analysis Climate Coaching Skills Participants will gain practical climate coaching skills, including the use of indigenous knowledge systems in climate adaptation and sustainability practices. National and Regional Visibility Outstanding Climate Champions may receive opportunities to showcase their work at regional and national events. Important Eligibility Information DOT Kenya has clarified that this opportunity is strictly open to new applicants only. Young people who have previously participated in or benefited from earlier DOT Kenya Cisco Climate Champions cohorts or projects are not eligible to apply. The organization is prioritizing youth who have not previously had access to Cisco-supported climate programming. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change remains one of the most pressing global challenges affecting livelihoods, food security, public health, and environmental sustainability. Young people are increasingly becoming central to climate advocacy and innovation worldwide. The DOT Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program recognizes the importance of empowering local youth leaders who understand the realities within their communities and can develop sustainable, locally relevant solutions. Participants will not only gain valuable technical and leadership experience but also contribute directly to environmental sustainability and community resilience efforts in Kenya. Application Deadline Applications for the Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026 close on May 26, 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to apply early because interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis during the application period, and positions may be filled before the official deadline. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . title: Multilingual AI for Health Challenge 2026: Build AI Systems for African Languages and Win Up to $5,000 USD
summary: Access to reliable healthcare information remains one of the biggest challenges across many African communities, particularly in underserved and low-resource settings where language barriers limit access to accurate medical guidance. To address this gap, the Multilingual Health Question Answering in Low-Resource African Languages Challenge has officially launched on Zindi, inviting AI innovators, developers, researchers, machine learning engineers, and language technology enthusiasts to build multilingual AI systems capable of answering health-related questions in African languages. The challenge is organized in collaboration with the Hub for Artificial Intelligence in Maternal, Sexual and Reproductive Health (HASH) and focuses specifically on maternal, sexual, and reproductive health information in African communities. Participants will work on creating AI models that can understand and generate accurate, fluent, and culturally appropriate responses in languages such as Akan, Kiswahili, Luganda, and Amharic. The competition offers a total prize pool of $5,000 USD and is fully online, making it accessible to participants from across the globe. Why This Challenge Matters Despite the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence and natural language processing technologies, most large language models are still heavily trained on English and other high-resource languages. As a result, millions of African language speakers remain underserved by AI-powered healthcare systems. This challenge seeks to change that reality by encouraging the development of multilingual health assistants capable of supporting African communities in their native languages. Such systems could eventually power: Rural health worker assistants Community healthcare support systems Maternal health education tools Adolescent sexual health platforms HIV and STI awareness systems Clinic support applications Patient education chatbots By enabling people to ask sensitive health questions privately and receive responses in their own language, these AI systems could significantly improve healthcare accessibility, awareness, and informed decision-making across Africa. About the Organizers The challenge is spearheaded by the Hub for Artificial Intelligence in Maternal , Sexual and Reproductive Health ( HASH ), a multidisciplinary consortium bringing together experts in healthcare, public health, artificial intelligence, data science, and social sciences. The consortium includes: Infectious Disease Institute (IDI) Makerere University Makerere Centre for Artificial Intelligence (MAK-AI) Sunbird AI HASH focuses on advancing AI-driven healthcare solutions across Sub-Saharan Africa, particularly in areas such as: Maternal health HIV care Sexually transmitted infections Adolescent sexual and reproductive health Their goal is to promote locally grounded AI innovations tailored specifically to African realities and healthcare systems. Challenge Objective Participants are required to build a multilingual AI model capable of: Understanding health-related questions posed in low-resource African languages Generating accurate and contextually appropriate answers Responding fluently in the same language as the question The challenge dataset consists of curated health question-and-answer pairs focused on maternal, sexual, and reproductive health. Competitors are encouraged to use open-source tools, publicly available pretrained models, and freely accessible datasets where applicable. Supported Skills and Technologies This challenge is especially suitable for individuals interested in: Large Language Models (LLMs) Natural Language Processing (NLP) Machine Translation Multilingual AI Low-resource language modeling Deep learning Healthcare AI systems Participants will gain hands-on experience building socially impactful AI technologies for African communities. Prize Pool The challenge offers the following prizes: 1st Prize — $2,500 USD 2nd Prize — $1,500 USD 3rd Prize — $1,000 USD Additionally, participants can earn: 5,000 Zindi points Recognition within the African AI ecosystem Opportunities for collaboration and visibility in AI for healthcare Evaluation Metrics Submissions will be evaluated using a multi-metric scoring system combining lexical overlap, structural similarity, and semantic judgment. ROUGE-1 F1 (37%) Measures overlap of individual words between prediction and reference answers. ROUGE-L F1 (37%) Measures sentence structure similarity using the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS). LLM-as-a-Judge (26%) Uses a language model to evaluate: Factual accuracy Completeness Language appropriateness Overall response quality Top-performing solutions will also undergo additional evaluation using AfroLM BertScore F1, which measures semantic similarity using embeddings from AfroLM : a multilingual transformer pretrained on 23 African languages. Important Rules and Requirements Participants should note the following: Maximum team size: 4 people Fully online participation Open-source tools only No paid services or proprietary tools allowed Automated machine learning tools are prohibited Maximum of 5 submissions per day Maximum of 50 submissions overall Top 10 teams must submit reproducible code and reports Final winners must publicly share their code and models The challenge also enforces strict anti-cheating policies, including penalties and disqualification for multiple accounts, unauthorized collaboration, or unreproducible submissions. Webinar Information Participants are encouraged to register for the official webinar scheduled for: Date: 20 May 2026 Time: 6:00 PM – 7:00 PM GMT+2 The webinar will likely provide guidance on: Dataset structure Evaluation methods Submission process Baseline models Best practices for multilingual healthcare AI systems Deadline Application Deadline: 21 July 2026 Why You Should Participate This challenge is more than a competition. It is an opportunity to contribute to the future of inclusive healthcare technology in Africa. By participating, you can: Build real-world AI solutions with social impact Strengthen your NLP and LLM expertise Gain exposure within the African AI ecosystem Collaborate with innovators across the continent Contribute to healthcare accessibility in underserved communities Compete for international recognition and cash prizes For students, researchers, startups, healthcare innovators, and machine learning engineers passionate about African language technologies, this challenge represents a rare opportunity to combine technical innovation with meaningful impact. Deadline 21st July ,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here.
